# fuji-geotag

> A small bash + exiftool wrapper that backfills GPS coordinates into camera
> files (RAW / HEIF / JPG) from a GPX track, **without destroying camera
> MakerNotes** (Film Simulation, Lens info, Dynamic Range, custom settings,
> etc.).

Default name is `fuji-geotag` because it was written for Fujifilm RAF/HIF
files, but the underlying technique (`exiftool -overwrite_original_in_place`)
works for any vendor: Sony ARW, Nikon NEF, Canon CR3, etc. Adjust `--exts`.

## The core technique

Naive `exiftool -geotag` rebuilds the file structure and can drop the
MakerNote block. The fix is a single flag:

```bash
exiftool -overwrite_original_in_place -P -m \
  -geotag=<gpx> -geosync=<tz> -api GeoMaxExtSecs=<n> <files>
```

`-overwrite_original_in_place` patches the existing bytes for the affected
tags only. Verified: MakerNote tag count before == after.

## Files

- [README.md](README.md): full documentation, options, examples
- [fuji-geotag](fuji-geotag): the script itself (single-file bash)
- [LICENSE](LICENSE): MIT

## Usage in one line

Installed:

```bash
fuji-geotag --gpx track.gpx --dir /path/to/photos --date 2026-06-01
```

Ad-hoc, no install:

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Innei/fuji-geotag/main/fuji-geotag \
  | bash -s -- --gpx track.gpx --dir . --date 2026-06-01
```

Defaults: looks for files missing GPS, filters by `DateTimeOriginal`, backs
up to `/tmp/fuji-geotag-backup-<ts>/`, runs the safe exiftool invocation,
verifies MakerNote tag count after.

## When to reach for it

- You shoot RAW + JPEG/HEIF on a camera without built-in GPS.
- You record a phone-side GPX track (Strava, Step Of My World, MotionX, etc.).
- You want GPS in EXIF without LightRoom / proprietary tooling.
- You've been burned before by exiftool stripping vendor MakerNotes.

## When *not* to use it

- You only want to view the location on a map — just open the GPX next to
  the photos in your DAM of choice.
- Your camera already writes GPS and you trust it.
- You need per-photo location editing — use a GUI tool.

## Requirements

- `exiftool` 12+ (`brew install exiftool`)
- `bash` 4+ (macOS users: brew bash, or any modern Linux)

## Repository

https://github.com/Innei/fuji-geotag
